items.title: Waanzinnige comeback in Championship, nieuwe stap Altena richting titel
items.summary: Met vier wedstrijden in de Premier League en een kraker in Coppa Italia beloofde het een volle voetbalavond te worden. Lees de volledige avond terug in onderstaande VI Live.

items.title: Como verrast Napoli en bereikt na veertig jaar weer halve finale Coppa Italia
items.summary: Como heeft zich na veertig jaar weer geplaatst voor de halve finale van de Coppa Italia. De ploeg van Cesc Fàbregas zorgde dinsdag voor een verrassing door Napoli na penalty's uit het Italiaanse bekertoernooi te kegelen.

items.title: Hertha sneuvelt na penalty's: Bundesliga-feestje in halve finale DFB-Pokal
items.summary: Hertha BSC heeft dinsdagavond op het nippertje naast de eerste halvefinaleplaats in de DFB-Pokal sinds 2016 gegrepen. SC Freiburg won na penalty's van de Tweede Bundesliga-club, waardoor er louter clubs uit de Bundesliga bij de laatste vier staan.

items.title: Haaland puts 'pressure on himself' to help team
items.summary: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says Erling Haaland puts "pressure on himself" to consistently score because he "desperately wants to help the team".

items.title: Premier League recap: West Ham deny Man United fifth straight win
items.summary: West Ham United ended Manchester United's winning streak, while Chelsea blew a 2-0 lead to draw against Leeds United in another eventful day of Premier League action.

items.title: Boos after Newcastle hand Tottenham another home defeat
items.summary: Tottenham fans are left frustrated as Newcastle inflict a seventh home defeat of the season on Thomas Frank's side.

items.title: Sesko scores SUPERB 96th-min equaliser as Man Utd's winning run ended
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from West Ham’s match against Manchester United in the Premier League.

items.title: Newcastle win at Spurs to pile pressure on Frank
items.summary: Jacob Ramsey fired Newcastle United to a precious 2-1 Premier League win to pile the pressure on Thomas Frank and increase Tottenham Hotspur 's relegation fears.

items.title: Late Sesko goal rescues United point at West Ham
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko scored in the sixth minute of stoppage time to rescue a point for Manchester United, whose winning run under Michael Carrick came to an end with a 1-1 draw at West Ham.

items.title: Report: Miami MLS' most valuable team at $1.45B
items.summary: Five clubs from MLS now have a valuation of over $1 billion, with Miami leading the way for the first time at $1.45 billion, according to a report published by Sportico on Tuesday.

items.title: Leeds mount comeback to take point at Chelsea
items.summary: Chelsea's promising start to life under Liam Rosenior was derailed as Leeds fought from two down to earn a 2-2 draw at Stamford Bridge and deny the hosts a fifth straight Premier League win.

items.title: Sesko rescues late point for Manchester United to deny West Ham precious win
items.summary: Stop all the clippers, don’t cut off the barnet: Manchester United’s winning run is over. Frank Ilett, the man who pledged never to visit a barber again until United won five matches in a row, has been denied a viral haircut, but their resilience came to the fore after the West Ham were denied what would have been a deserved victory deep into added time. A studded finish from fan favourite Tomas Soucek was cancelled out by a strike from Benjamin Sesko to leave this match honours even. United lacked energy and snap, West Ham thought they had the game in the bag as the clock ticked past 90 minutes. The Hammers remain in 18th place, five points behind Spurs in 16th. Continue reading...

items.title: Sesko voorkomt diep in blessuretijd eerste nederlaag van Carrick
items.summary: Manchester United heeft na vier gewonnen duels op rij weer eens punten laten liggen. De ploeg van Michael Carrick gleed dinsdagavond uit bij West Ham United, al zal het voor hem als een gewonnen punt aanvoelen, omdat de gelijkmaker diep in blessuretijd viel.

items.title: Man Utd-fan Frank Ilett kan na 493 dagen nog steeds niet naar de kapper
items.summary: Frank Ilett hoopte na 493 dagen eindelijk weer een afspraak te kunnen maken bij de kapper, maar de supporter van Manchester United kwam bedrogen uit. Door het puntenverlies van The Red Devils bij West Ham United is de veelbesproken social media-actie van Ilett voorlopig nog niet voorbij.

items.title: Sesko scores 96th-minute equaliser to deny West Ham vital victory
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko came off the bench to deny West Ham a vital win in their hunt for Premier League survival as his 96th-minute goal earned a 1-1 draw for Manchester United at the London Stadium.

items.title: Villa plotting Gibbs-White move - Wednesday's gossip
items.summary: Aston Villa are plotting a move for Morgan Gibbs-White, Liverpool's Mohamed Salah is a target for one of the Saudi Pro League's top clubs, Sandro Tonali's wages could be an obstacle for Serie A clubs, plus more.

items.title: Magnusson scores late winner as Hearts move six points clear at the summit
items.summary: Substitute Tomas Magnusson scored a late winner as Scottish Premiership leaders Hearts defeated Hibernian 1-0 in a tense Edinburgh derby to tighten their grip on top spot.

items.title: Okafor grabs comeback draw for Leeds as wasteful Chelsea’s bad habits return
items.summary: It turns out Chelsea’s unfortunate habit of squandering winning positions did not leave with Enzo Maresca. They remain infuriatingly inconsistent and were at their careless worst against a defiant Leeds United, who took another step towards staying up by fighting back from 2-0 down to leave Stamford Bridge with a gritty point. Liam Rosenior was left wondering why the focus was not on Cole Palmer collecting his first assist in the Premier League this season. Chelsea’s new head coach was on his way to an eighth win in his first 10 games in charge. Instead he watched in disbelief as his team fell apart, gifting Lukas Nmecha a penalty before more dreadful defending allowed Noah Okafor to haul Daniel Farke’s Leeds level. Continue reading...

items.title: Rayan and Adli stage Bournemouth fightback against 10-man Everton
items.summary: So much for the comforts of home. Everton had a tantalising opportunity at Hill Dickinson Stadium with sixth place and momentum in the push for European qualification the prize for beating Bournemouth. David Moyes’s team let it slip through their grasp once again. Andoni Iraola’s side extended their unbeaten Premier League run to six games courtesy of second-half headers from Rayan, who could just be their latest gem, and Amine Adli. Bournemouth’s brace, plus a straight red card for the Everton defender Jake O’Brien, arrived in an eight-minute spell in which the hosts imploded to leave themselves without a home win since 6 December. Everton have collected 17 points from their last eight away games but only eight from the last nine at home. Their quest for Europe is floundering on home soil. Continue reading...

items.title: Ramsey winner for Newcastle adds to Frank woes to leave Spurs fearing the drop
items.summary: To his extensive list of problems at Tottenham, Thomas Frank can add another. The manager is dealing with a ghost from the club’s more successful recent past. There were 49 minutes on the clock when the South Stand choir took up the song. “He’s magic, you know. Mauricio Poch-e-ttino.” The message was clear. They want their former idol back. They cannot persist any longer with Frank. The only wonder here was that Newcastle, who had arrived on the back of three straight Premier League defeats, with numerous issues of their own, were not out of sight at the interval. If they were bright in the first half, Spurs were impossibly awful. Frank’s injury list was in double figures and he would lose another player – Wilson Odobert – in the 34th minute. No one wants to hear the mitigation. Continue reading...
